stories.text: Squawk Box host Andrew Ross Sorkin lets Brooke Rollins get away with blaming high grocery costs on Biden. It's almost like there was an agreement before this interview to not ask any follow up questions. Fox News lite.

stories.author: Judd Legum
stories.title: PopInfo Weekly: Bankrolls, blackouts, and body cams
stories.text: Since the start of 2025, 110 House members and 37 senators have sponsored or cosponsored 40 bills to expand the power of U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE). Popular Information identified the corporate donors who have provided the most support to these politicians, which include SpaceX, Home Depot, Walmart, and Google. After two fatal shootings by ICE agents in Texas and Maine, during which officers were not wearing body cams, White House Border Czar Tom Homan defended the agency. He said Democrats held up the purchase of body cams and “as soon as [ICE] had funding they bought them.” That funding was provided in April. Documents uncovered by Popular Information show that it was not until July 17 and 20, days after Homan claimed the purchases had already been made, that ICE spent over $30 million on body cams. On May 26, the Food and Drug Administration sent a letter to pharmaceutical company QOL Medical with serious allegations that could pose an existential threat to the company. Days later, QOL Medical CEO Frederick Cooper wrote a $1 million check to Trump’s super PAC, MAGA Inc. On Wednesday, the Trump administration announced a nuclear agreement with Saudi Arabia tha

stories.author: Marisa Kabas
stories.title: two people were stabbed in NYC today by a man who reportedly yelled ‘allahu albar’ during the attacks. one vic...
stories.text: two people were stabbed in NYC today by a man who reportedly yelled ‘allahu albar’ during the attacks. one victim was jewish. the other was asian. fortunately both are expected to survive. it’s telling how in much of the coverage, only one victim’s identity is important and “explains” what happened.

stories.author: Ken Klippenstein
stories.title: Exclusive: Francesca Hong Takes on National Security
stories.text: Francesca Hong’s insurgent campaign for governor of Wisconsin (where I live) echoes Zohran Mamdani’s rise, but with a striking difference. She has something to say about national security. Hong, 37, is a state legislator and chef based in Madison, where she was born and raised. The Democratic Socialists of America member entered the race for governor last September and has consistently led in the polls since March. Whereas the New York mayor, for all his boldness in other areas, has largely avoided the issue of national security, Hong is facing it head on. I sent her a detailed questionnaire about her thinking on the Iran war, the president’s national security directive NSPM-7 , and Wisconsin’s role in the war machine, and she came back with lengthy answers. On an issue that many assume their governor has no role to play, Hong has proven otherwise. Most striking is her vow of a showdown with President Trump over the Wisconsin National Guard. As Hong told me: “The focus of this campaign is on improving the lives of working people and families. That said, we do not live in a vacuum. As governor, I will tirelessly defend the people of this state against egregious federal encroachment

stories.author: Drop Site team
stories.title: Prosecutor Investigating U.S. Boat Strikes and Claims of Torture Was Murdered in Ecuador
stories.text: This is essential to our mission: to report on what matters most, beholden only to the truth. In that spirit, we made a commitment to ensure that our journalism is free for everyone, not locked behind a paywall. But that means we rely on the voluntary support of our community of readers. Support Drop Site With a Gift Today Crime scene of prosecutor Alexandra Bravo’s and her sister, Olinda Bravo’s assassination. Photo provided to Drop Site News by Radio Pichincha. Para leer esta historia en español, haga clic aquí. QUITO, Ecuador—Alexandra Bravo, the prosecutor assigned to investigate U.S. strikes on three Ecuadorian boats and the 36 survivors’ subsequent accounts of torture and kidnapping, was shot dead in the coastal city of Manta last month. According to police, on June 14 a hitman aboard a black motorcycle opened fire on Bravo as she and her sister were leaving a café at about 11 a.m. Bravo’s sister, who jumped to protect her, was also killed in the attack. Upon arriving at the crime scene, officers say they found five 9mm shell casings and Bravo’s injured driver, but no sign of the police detail that had been assigned to protect her. The Police Directorate, which operates entir

stories.author: Drop Site team
stories.title: Asesinan A La Fiscal Que Investigaba Los Ataques Estadounidenses Contra Pescadores Ecuatorianos y Denuncias De Tortura
stories.text: Escena del crimen del asesinato de la fiscal Alexandra Bravo y su hermana, Olinda Bravo. Foto proporcionada a Drop Site News por Radio Pichincha. To read this story in English, click here. QUITO, Ecuador—Alexandra Bravo, la fiscal encargada de investigar los ataques de EE. UU. contra tres embarcaciones ecuatorianas y los relatos de tortura y secuestro de los 36 sobrevivientes, fue asesinada a tiros en la ciudad costera de Manta el mes pasado. Según la policía, el 14 de junio un sicario a bordo de una motocicleta negra disparó contra Bravo cuando ella y su hermana salían de una cafetería alrededor de las 11 de la mañana. La hermana de Bravo, quien se lanzó a protegerla, también murió en el ataque. Al llegar a la escena del crimen, los agentes dicen que encontraron cinco casquillos de 9 mm y al chofer de Bravo herido, pero ningún rastro de la escolta policial que había sido asignada para protegerla. La Policía Nacional, que opera íntegramente bajo el poder ejecutivo, ha iniciado una investigación para determinar si hubo una falla de seguridad interna. Bravo es la vigésimo sexta miembro del sistema judicial de Ecuador asesinada desde 2020 y la segunda persona que investigaba delitos d

stories.title: The SNAP Subsidy
stories.text: This is a web version of The Lever ’s daily email newsletter. 📹 First watch: One corporation controls 35 percent of all bowling revenues. 👀 Now read: Meet the kingpin behind private equity’s takeover of your local lanes. 👇 Further down in this newsletter: Which employers are relying on SNAP to subsidize wages. stories.author: Drop Site team
stories.title: Ansarallah strikes two Saudi tankers; Oil surges past $100; Over 4,200 Israeli settlers storm Al-Aqsa
stories.text: Thursday, July 23, 2026 – U.S. targets Iran for 12th straight night. Iran launches retaliatory attacks. IRGC reports interceptions of tankers. Damaged tanker towed into Iranian waters. Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammed Bagher Ghalibaf says there will be no oil sales in the Gulf until Iran’s security is assured. Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi threatens retaliation for any support for infrastructure attacks. Report: U.S. tells Israel it will intensify strikes on Iran. U.S. probes possible Russian role in Iranian drone strikes on CIA sites. Ansarallah strikes two tankers in the Red Sea. President Donald Trump threatens Iran over future Ansarallah attacks. Clashes in Southern Yemen. Israel continues its assault on Gaza. Two Palestinians killed near Nablus; Israel alleges stabbing attack. Israeli attacks on the occupied West Bank. Over 4,200 settlers storm Al-Aqsa compound. Malaysia rejects U.S. pressure to reverse its ban on Israeli citizens. Israeli military conducts multiple operations in Lebanon amid “ceasefire.” Oil surges past $100. Amish Shah wins Arizona House primary. House narrowly passes $95 billion budget for Iran war. stories.author: Judd Legum
stories.title: 1. Trump just signed a landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia, paving the way for the Saudis to enrich their ...
stories.text: 1. Trump just signed a landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia, paving the way for the Saudis to enrich their own uranium. Meanwhile, Trump's top foreign policy advisor, Jared Kushner, is being paid tens of millions of dollars anually by the Saudis That fact was IGNORED in almost all the coverage

stories.author: Judd Legum
stories.title: A nuclear deal for the Saudis. A media blackout for Kushner’s corruption.
stories.text: The Saudi government has been aggressively seeking such a deal for years , eager to gain an edge over regional rivals. The agreement lacks safeguards that previous administrations, and many independent foreign policy analysts, viewed as essential to prevent nuclear proliferation. The deal comes after the Saudi government’s Public Investment Fund (PIF) invested $2 billion in Jared Kushner’s private equity fund, Affinity Partners. Since 2021, Kushner, President Trump’s son-in-law, has collected over $110 million from the Saudi government in management fees. In March, Kushner was seeking billions more from the Saudis . (After Kushner’s fundraising was exposed, his efforts were purportedly put on hold.) Kushner also has a close personal relationship with Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman (MBS) — the pair are known to communicate regularly over WhatsApp. In 2021, when the PIF committee originally recommended rejecting Kushner’s proposal due to the high price and Kushner’s inexperience, MBS overruled the recommendation. As he continues to collect fees from the Saudi government, Kushner has resumed his role as a top foreign policy aide to Trump. He has played a key role in virtually

stories.author: Bellingcat
stories.title: Open source tools can help track every stage of a wildfire, from the first reports through to assessing the da...
stories.text: Open source tools can help track every stage of a wildfire, from the first reports through to assessing the damage once the flames are out. Throughout July, the Copernicus Atmosphere Monitoring Service helped researchers monitor how wildfire smoke spread across Canada and the US.
